title: FOXAI Dirty Python Lab V1 — Prompt Generate Run Capture Repair

changes.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import threading
import time
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import messagebox
from tkinter.scrolledtext import ScrolledText

from model_adapter import ModelAdapter
from runner import PythonRunner, RunResult


LAB_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
WORKSPACE = LAB_ROOT / "workspace"
RUNS = LAB_ROOT / "runs"
MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3
DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 12.0


class DirtyPythonLabApp:
    BG = "#111318"
    PANEL = "#1B1E26"
    TEXT_BG = "#090B0F"
    FG = "#F5F5F5"
    ACCENT = "#9C6CFF"
    SECONDARY = "#2D3340"
    WARNING = "#F3C969"

    def __init__(self, root: tk.Tk) -> None:
        self.root = root
        self.root.title("FOXAI Dirty Python Lab V1")
        self.root.geometry("1500x900")
        self.root.minsize(1100, 700)
        self.root.configure(bg=self.BG)

        WORKSPACE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        RUNS.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        self.adapter = ModelAdapter()
        self.runner = PythonRunner(sys.executable)
        self.stop_event = threading.Event()
        self.busy = False
        self._self_test_process: subprocess.Popen[str] | None = None

        self.status_var = tk.StringVar(value="READY")
        self._build_ui()
        self.root.protocol("WM_DELETE_WINDOW", self._on_close)

    def _build_ui(self) -> None:
        title = tk.Label(
            self.root,
            text="FOXAI DIRTY PYTHON LAB",
            font=("Segoe UI", 24, "bold"),
            bg=self.BG,
            fg=self.FG,
        )
        title.pack(pady=(14, 6))

        prompt_label = tk.Label(
            self.root,
            text="Describe the Python tool you want:",
            font=("Segoe UI", 18, "bold"),
            bg=self.BG,
            fg=self.FG,
        )
        prompt_label.pack(anchor="w", padx=20)

        self.prompt_box = ScrolledText(
            self.root,
            height=6,
            wrap="word",
            font=("Segoe UI", 18),
            bg=self.TEXT_BG,
            fg=self.FG,
            insertbackground=self.FG,
            selectbackground=self.ACCENT,
            relief="flat",
            padx=12,
            pady=10,
        )
        self.prompt_box.pack(fill="x", padx=20, pady=(6, 10))
        self.prompt_box.insert(
            "1.0",
            "Write a Python program that prints the numbers 1 through 10 and their total.",
        )

        buttons = tk.Frame(self.root, bg=self.BG)
        buttons.pack(fill="x", padx=20, pady=4)
        for column in range(5):
            buttons.grid_columnconfigure(column, weight=1)

        self.generate_button = self._button(
            buttons, "GENERATE AND TEST", self.start_generate, self.ACCENT
        )
        self.stop_button = self._button(
            buttons, "STOP", self.stop_current, "#8E3940"
        )
        self.open_button = self._button(
            buttons, "OPEN WORKSPACE", self.open_workspace, self.SECONDARY
        )
        self.reset_button = self._button(
            buttons, "RESET WORKSPACE", self.reset_workspace, self.SECONDARY
        )
        self.test_button = self._button(
            buttons, "RUN SELF TESTS", self.start_self_tests, self.SECONDARY
        )
        for index, button in enumerate(
            [
                self.generate_button,
                self.stop_button,
                self.open_button,
                self.reset_button,
                self.test_button,
            ]
        ):
            button.grid(row=0, column=index, sticky="ew", padx=5, pady=5)

        status_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg=self.PANEL)
        status_frame.pack(fill="x", padx=20, pady=(8, 10))
        tk.Label(
            status_frame,
            text="STATUS:",
            font=("Segoe UI", 18, "bold"),
            bg=self.PANEL,
            fg=self.WARNING,
        ).pack(side="left", padx=(12, 8), pady=10)
        self.status_label = tk.Label(
            status_frame,
            textvariable=self.status_var,
            font=("Segoe UI", 20, "bold"),
            bg=self.PANEL,
            fg=self.FG,
            anchor="w",
        )
        self.status_label.pack(side="left", fill="x", expand=True, padx=(0, 12), pady=10)

        panes = tk.PanedWindow(
            self.root,
            orient="horizontal",
            sashwidth=8,
            bg=self.BG,
            bd=0,
        )
        panes.pack(fill="both", expand=True, padx=20, pady=(0, 18))

        code_frame = self._text_panel(panes, "GENERATED PYTHON")
        result_frame = self._text_panel(panes, "EXECUTION RESULT")
        panes.add(code_frame, minsize=450)
        panes.add(result_frame, minsize=450)

    def _button(self, parent: tk.Widget, text: str, command, color: str) -> tk.Button:
        return tk.Button(
            parent,
            text=text,
            command=command,
            font=("Segoe UI", 15, "bold"),
            bg=color,
            fg=self.FG,
            activebackground=color,
            activeforeground=self.FG,
            relief="flat",
            padx=10,
            pady=13,
            cursor="hand2",
        )

    def _text_panel(self, parent: tk.Widget, label: str) -> tk.Frame:
        frame = tk.Frame(parent, bg=self.PANEL)
        tk.Label(
            frame,
            text=label,
            font=("Segoe UI", 17, "bold"),
            bg=self.PANEL,
            fg=self.FG,
        ).pack(anchor="w", padx=10, pady=(8, 3))
        widget = ScrolledText(
            frame,
            wrap="none",
            font=("Consolas", 16),
            bg=self.TEXT_BG,
            fg=self.FG,
            insertbackground=self.FG,
            selectbackground=self.ACCENT,
            relief="flat",
            padx=10,
            pady=10,
        )
        widget.pack(fill="both", expand=True, padx=8, pady=(0, 8))
        if label.startswith("GENERATED"):
            self.code_box = widget
        else:
            self.result_box = widget
        return frame

    def _ui(self, callback) -> None:
        self.root.after(0, callback)

    def set_status(self, text: str) -> None:
        self._ui(lambda: self.status_var.set(text))

    @staticmethod
    def _replace_text(widget: tk.Text, text: str) -> None:
        widget.delete("1.0", "end")
        widget.insert("1.0", text)
        widget.see("1.0")

    def show_code(self, text: str) -> None:
        self._ui(lambda: self._replace_text(self.code_box, text))

    def show_result(self, text: str) -> None:
        self._ui(lambda: self._replace_text(self.result_box, text))

    def _set_busy(self, busy: bool) -> None:
        self.busy = busy
        state = "disabled" if busy else "normal"
        self._ui(lambda: self.generate_button.configure(state=state))
        self._ui(lambda: self.reset_button.configure(state=state))
        self._ui(lambda: self.test_button.configure(state=state))

    def start_generate(self) -> None:
        if self.busy:
            return
        prompt = self.prompt_box.get("1.0", "end").strip()
        if not prompt:
            messagebox.showinfo("Dirty Python Lab", "Type a plain-language Python request first.")
            return
        self.stop_event.clear()
        self.adapter.reset_cancel()
        self._set_busy(True)
        threading.Thread(target=self._workflow, args=(prompt,), daemon=True).start()

    def _workflow(self, prompt: str) -> None:
        run_id = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S_") + os.urandom(2).hex().upper()
        run_dir = RUNS / run_id
        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
        WORKSPACE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        (run_dir / "request.txt").write_text(prompt, encoding="utf-8")
        (WORKSPACE / "prompt.txt").write_text(prompt, encoding="utf-8")

        attempts: list[dict] = []
        previous_program = ""
        previous_result: dict = {}
        final_status = "FAILED"
        final_model = "local-model"

        try:
            for attempt_number in range(1, MAX_ATTEMPTS + 1):
                if self.stop_event.is_set():
                    final_status = "STOPPED"
                    break

                attempt_dir = run_dir / f"attempt_{attempt_number:02d}"
                attempt_dir.mkdir()
                if attempt_number == 1:
                    self.set_status(f"GENERATING ATTEMPT {attempt_number} OF {MAX_ATTEMPTS}")
                    reply = self.adapter.generate_program(prompt)
                    repair_prompt = ""
                else:
                    self.set_status(f"REPAIRING ATTEMPT {attempt_number} OF {MAX_ATTEMPTS}")
                    reply, repair_prompt = self.adapter.repair_program(
                        prompt,
                        previous_program,
                        previous_result,
                        attempt_number,
                        MAX_ATTEMPTS,
                    )
                    (attempt_dir / "repair_prompt.txt").write_text(
                        repair_prompt, encoding="utf-8"
                    )

                final_model = reply.model
                (attempt_dir / "model_response.txt").write_text(
                    reply.text if reply.ok else reply.error,
                    encoding="utf-8",
                )
                (WORKSPACE / "last_model_response.txt").write_text(
                    reply.text if reply.ok else reply.error,
                    encoding="utf-8",
                )
                if not reply.ok:
                    final_status = "STOPPED" if self.stop_event.is_set() else "MODEL OFFLINE"
                    self.set_status(final_status)
                    self.show_result(
                        f"{final_status}\n\n{reply.error}\n\n"
                        "Start FOXAI Chat Engine if it is offline, then press GENERATE AND TEST again."
                    )
                    attempts.append(
                        {
                            "attempt": attempt_number,
                            "model_ok": False,
                            "model_error": reply.error,
                        }
                    )
                    break

                program = self.adapter.extract_candidate(reply.text)
                previous_program = program
                (attempt_dir / "candidate.py").write_text(
                    program, encoding="utf-8", newline="\n"
                )
                (WORKSPACE / "candidate.py").write_text(
                    program, encoding="utf-8", newline="\n"
                )
                self.show_code(program)
                self.set_status(f"RUNNING ATTEMPT {attempt_number} OF {MAX_ATTEMPTS}")

                result = self.runner.run_source(
                    program,
                    attempt_dir,
                    timeout_seconds=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
                    stop_event=self.stop_event,
                )
                previous_result = result.to_dict()
                (WORKSPACE / "result.json").write_text(
                    json.dumps(previous_result, indent=2),
                    encoding="utf-8",
                )
                attempts.append(
                    {
                        "attempt": attempt_number,
                        "model_ok": True,
                        "model": reply.model,
                        "result": previous_result,
                    }
                )
                self.show_result(self.format_result(result, attempt_number))
                self.set_status(result.status)

                if result.success:
                    final_status = "SUCCESS"
                    break
                if result.stopped:
                    final_status = "STOPPED"
                    break
                final_status = result.status

            else:
                final_status = "FAILED"

            receipt = {
                "schema": "foxai.dirty_python_lab.run.v1",
                "run_id": run_id,
                "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
                "original_prompt": prompt,
                "status": final_status,
                "model": final_model,
                "max_attempts": MAX_ATTEMPTS,
                "attempts": attempts,
                "lab_root": str(LAB_ROOT),
                "production_files_modified_by_lab": False,
                "automatic_package_installation": False,
            }
            (run_dir / "run_receipt.json").write_text(
                json.dumps(receipt, indent=2), encoding="utf-8"
            )
            (run_dir / "final_status.txt").write_text(
                final_status + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
            )
            self.set_status(final_status)
        except Exception as error:
            final_status = "FAILED"
            self.set_status(final_status)
            self.show_result(f"FAILED\n\n{type(error).__name__}: {error}")
        finally:
            self._set_busy(False)

    @staticmethod
    def format_result(result: RunResult, attempt_number: int) -> str:
        return (
            f"{result.status}\n"
            f"Attempt: {attempt_number} of {MAX_ATTEMPTS}\n"
            f"Phase: {result.phase}\n"
            f"Return code: {result.return_code}\n"
            f"Timed out: {result.timed_out}\n"
            f"Stopped: {result.stopped}\n"
            f"Elapsed: {result.elapsed_seconds:.3f} seconds\n\n"
            f"STDOUT\n{'-' * 60}\n{result.stdout or '[empty]'}\n\n"
            f"STDERR\n{'-' * 60}\n{result.stderr or '[empty]'}"
        )

    def stop_current(self) -> None:
        self.stop_event.set()
        self.adapter.cancel()
        self.runner.stop_current()
        process = self._self_test_process
        if process is not None and process.poll() is None:
            try:
                if os.name == "nt":
                    subprocess.run(
                        ["taskkill", "/PID", str(process.pid), "/T", "/F"],
                        shell=False,
                        stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                        stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                        creationflags=int(getattr(subprocess, "CREATE_NO_WINDOW", 0)),
                        check=False,
                    )
                else:
                    process.kill()
            except Exception:
                pass
        self.set_status("STOPPED")

    def open_workspace(self) -> None:
        WORKSPACE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        try:
            if os.name == "nt":
                os.startfile(str(WORKSPACE))  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            elif sys.platform == "darwin":
                subprocess.Popen(["open", str(WORKSPACE)])
            else:
                subprocess.Popen(["xdg-open", str(WORKSPACE)])
        except Exception as error:
            messagebox.showerror("Open Workspace", f"Could not open workspace:\n{error}")

    def reset_workspace(self) -> None:
        if self.busy:
            messagebox.showinfo("Dirty Python Lab", "Stop the current run before resetting.")
            return
        try:
            if WORKSPACE.exists():
                shutil.rmtree(WORKSPACE)
            WORKSPACE.mkdir(parents=True)
            self.show_code("")
            self.show_result("Workspace reset. Historical runs were preserved.")
            self.set_status("READY")
        except Exception as error:
            messagebox.showerror("Reset Workspace", str(error))

    def start_self_tests(self) -> None:
        if self.busy:
            return
        self.stop_event.clear()
        self._set_busy(True)
        self.set_status("RUNNING SELF TESTS")
        self.show_result("Running deterministic runner tests and model tests when available...")
        threading.Thread(target=self._run_self_tests, daemon=True).start()

    def _run_self_tests(self) -> None:
        command = [
            sys.executable,
            "-B",
            str(LAB_ROOT / "acceptance_tests.py"),
            "--with-model",
        ]
        try:
            self._self_test_process = subprocess.Popen(
                command,
                cwd=str(LAB_ROOT),
                shell=False,
                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
                stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
                text=True,
                encoding="utf-8",
                errors="replace",
                creationflags=(
                    int(getattr(subprocess, "CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP", 0))
                    | int(getattr(subprocess, "CREATE_NO_WINDOW", 0))
                    if os.name == "nt"
                    else 0
                ),
            )
            output, _ = self._self_test_process.communicate(timeout=360)
            code = self._self_test_process.returncode
            self.show_result(output)
            self.set_status("SELF TESTS PASSED" if code == 0 else "SELF TESTS FAILED")
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            self.stop_current()
            self.show_result("SELF TESTS TIMED OUT")
            self.set_status("TIMED OUT")
        except Exception as error:
            self.show_result(f"SELF TESTS FAILED\n\n{type(error).__name__}: {error}")
            self.set_status("SELF TESTS FAILED")
        finally:
            self._self_test_process = None
            self._set_busy(False)

    def _on_close(self) -> None:
        self.stop_current()
        self.root.after(150, self.root.destroy)


def self_check() -> int:
    WORKSPACE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    RUNS.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    expected = [
        LAB_ROOT / "dirty_python_lab.py",
        LAB_ROOT / "runner.py",
        LAB_ROOT / "model_adapter.py",
        LAB_ROOT / "acceptance_tests.py",
        LAB_ROOT / "START_DIRTY_PYTHON_LAB.bat",
        LAB_ROOT / "README_EASY.txt",
    ]
    missing = [str(path) for path in expected if not path.is_file()]
    result = {
        "status": "PASS" if not missing else "FAIL",
        "lab_root": str(LAB_ROOT),
        "python": sys.executable,
        "workspace": str(WORKSPACE),
        "runs": str(RUNS),
        "model_api": ModelAdapter().api_url,
        "missing": missing,
    }
    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
    return 0 if not missing else 1


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--self-check", action="store_true")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    if args.self_check:
        return self_check()
    root = tk.Tk()
    DirtyPythonLabApp(root)
    root.mainloop()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
import signal
import subprocess
import sys
import threading
import time
import traceback
from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Callable


@dataclass
class RunResult:
    status: str
    success: bool
    phase: str
    return_code: int | None
    stdout: str
    stderr: str
    timed_out: bool
    stopped: bool
    elapsed_seconds: float
    command: list[str]
    working_directory: str
    candidate_path: str

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return asdict(self)


class PythonRunner:
    """Execute one generated Python file in a disposable attempt folder."""

    MAX_CAPTURE_CHARS = 200_000

    def __init__(self, python_executable: str | Path | None = None) -> None:
        self.python_executable = Path(python_executable or sys.executable).resolve()
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._current_process: subprocess.Popen[str] | None = None

    @staticmethod
    def _format_syntax_error(error: SyntaxError) -> str:
        return "".join(traceback.format_exception_only(type(error), error)).rstrip()

    @staticmethod
    def _windows_creation_flags() -> int:
        if os.name != "nt":
            return 0
        flags = 0
        flags |= int(getattr(subprocess, "CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP", 0))
        flags |= int(getattr(subprocess, "CREATE_NO_WINDOW", 0))
        return flags

    def _terminate_process_tree(self, process: subprocess.Popen[str]) -> None:
        if process.poll() is not None:
            return
        if os.name == "nt":
            try:
                subprocess.run(
                    ["taskkill", "/PID", str(process.pid), "/T", "/F"],
                    shell=False,
                    stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                    stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                    timeout=5,
                    creationflags=int(getattr(subprocess, "CREATE_NO_WINDOW", 0)),
                    check=False,
                )
            except Exception:
                pass
        else:
            try:
                os.killpg(os.getpgid(process.pid), signal.SIGKILL)
            except Exception:
                pass
        try:
            process.kill()
        except Exception:
            pass

    def stop_current(self) -> bool:
        with self._lock:
            process = self._current_process
        if process is None or process.poll() is not None:
            return False
        self._terminate_process_tree(process)
        return True

    @classmethod
    def _cap(cls, value: str) -> str:
        if len(value) <= cls.MAX_CAPTURE_CHARS:
            return value
        return value[: cls.MAX_CAPTURE_CHARS] + "\n\n[OUTPUT TRUNCATED BY DIRTY PYTHON LAB]"

    def run_source(
        self,
        source: str,
        attempt_dir: str | Path,
        *,
        timeout_seconds: float = 12.0,
        stop_event: threading.Event | None = None,
        status_callback: Callable[[str], None] | None = None,
    ) -> RunResult:
        attempt_path = Path(attempt_dir).resolve()
        attempt_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        candidate = attempt_path / "candidate.py"
        candidate.write_text(source, encoding="utf-8", newline="\n")

        started = time.perf_counter()
        try:
            compile(source, str(candidate), "exec")
        except SyntaxError as error:
            elapsed = time.perf_counter() - started
            result = RunResult(
                status="FAILED",
                success=False,
                phase="syntax",
                return_code=1,
                stdout="",
                stderr=self._format_syntax_error(error),
                timed_out=False,
                stopped=False,
                elapsed_seconds=round(elapsed, 4),
                command=[str(self.python_executable), "-I", "-B", str(candidate)],
                working_directory=str(attempt_path),
                candidate_path=str(candidate),
            )
            self.write_result(attempt_path, result)
            return result

        command = [str(self.python_executable), "-I", "-B", str(candidate)]
        environment = os.environ.copy()
        environment.pop("PYTHONPATH", None)
        environment.pop("PYTHONHOME", None)
        environment["PYTHONNOUSERSITE"] = "1"
        environment["PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE"] = "1"
        temp_dir = attempt_path / "temp"
        temp_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        environment["TEMP"] = str(temp_dir)
        environment["TMP"] = str(temp_dir)
        environment["TMPDIR"] = str(temp_dir)
        environment["FOXAI_DIRTY_LAB_ROOT"] = str(attempt_path)

        if status_callback:
            status_callback("RUNNING")

        process: subprocess.Popen[str] | None = None
        stdout = ""
        stderr = ""
        timed_out = False
        stopped = False
        try:
            process = subprocess.Popen(
                command,
                cwd=str(attempt_path),
                env=environment,
                shell=False,
                stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
                text=True,
                encoding="utf-8",
                errors="replace",
                creationflags=self._windows_creation_flags(),
                start_new_session=(os.name != "nt"),
            )
            with self._lock:
                self._current_process = process

            deadline = time.monotonic() + max(0.1, float(timeout_seconds))
            while True:
                if stop_event is not None and stop_event.is_set():
                    stopped = True
                    self._terminate_process_tree(process)
                    break
                remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
                if remaining <= 0:
                    timed_out = True
                    self._terminate_process_tree(process)
                    break
                try:
                    stdout, stderr = process.communicate(timeout=min(0.2, remaining))
                    break
                except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
                    continue

            if process.poll() is None:
                self._terminate_process_tree(process)
            try:
                tail_out, tail_err = process.communicate(timeout=3)
                stdout = stdout or tail_out or ""
                stderr = stderr or tail_err or ""
            except Exception:
                pass

            return_code = process.returncode
            if stopped:
                status = "STOPPED"
                success = False
                phase = "execution"
                stderr = (stderr + "\nStopped by the operator.").strip()
            elif timed_out:
                status = "TIMED OUT"
                success = False
                phase = "execution"
                stderr = (
                    stderr
                    + f"\nExecution exceeded the {timeout_seconds:g}-second timeout."
                ).strip()
            elif return_code == 0:
                status = "SUCCESS"
                success = True
                phase = "execution"
            else:
                status = "FAILED"
                success = False
                phase = "execution"

            result = RunResult(
                status=status,
                success=success,
                phase=phase,
                return_code=return_code,
                stdout=self._cap(stdout or ""),
                stderr=self._cap(stderr or ""),
                timed_out=timed_out,
                stopped=stopped,
                elapsed_seconds=round(time.perf_counter() - started, 4),
                command=command,
                working_directory=str(attempt_path),
                candidate_path=str(candidate),
            )
            self.write_result(attempt_path, result)
            return result
        except Exception as error:
            if process is not None:
                self._terminate_process_tree(process)
            result = RunResult(
                status="FAILED",
                success=False,
                phase="runner",
                return_code=None,
                stdout=self._cap(stdout),
                stderr=f"{type(error).__name__}: {error}",
                timed_out=False,
                stopped=bool(stop_event and stop_event.is_set()),
                elapsed_seconds=round(time.perf_counter() - started, 4),
                command=command,
                working_directory=str(attempt_path),
                candidate_path=str(candidate),
            )
            self.write_result(attempt_path, result)
            return result
        finally:
            with self._lock:
                if self._current_process is process:
                    self._current_process = None

    @staticmethod
    def write_result(attempt_path: Path, result: RunResult) -> None:
        (attempt_path / "result.json").write_text(
            json.dumps(result.to_dict(), indent=2),
            encoding="utf-8",
        )

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import hashlib
import json
import os
import shutil
import sys
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

from model_adapter import ModelAdapter
from runner import PythonRunner


LAB_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
RUNS = LAB_ROOT / "runs"
EXPECTED_FILES = {
    "dirty_python_lab.py",
    "runner.py",
    "model_adapter.py",
    "acceptance_tests.py",
    "START_DIRTY_PYTHON_LAB.bat",
    "README_EASY.txt",
}


def tree_fingerprint(path: Path) -> str:
    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    if not path.exists():
        digest.update(b"[missing]")
        return digest.hexdigest()
    for item in sorted(path.rglob("*"), key=lambda p: str(p).lower()):
        try:
            relative = item.relative_to(path).as_posix()
            stat = item.stat()
            kind = "D" if item.is_dir() else "F"
            digest.update(
                f"{kind}|{relative}|{stat.st_size}|{stat.st_mtime_ns}\n".encode(
                    "utf-8", errors="replace"
                )
            )
        except OSError:
            continue
    return digest.hexdigest()


def protected_fingerprints() -> dict[str, str]:
    foxai_root = LAB_ROOT.parents[1]
    candidates = {
        "core": foxai_root / "core",
        "ui": foxai_root / "ui",
        "site_packages": foxai_root / "env" / "python" / "Lib" / "site-packages",
    }
    return {name: tree_fingerprint(path) for name, path in candidates.items()}


def result_line(name: str, status: str, detail: str) -> str:
    return f"{status}: {name} — {detail}"


def deterministic_tests(root: Path) -> list[dict]:
    runner = PythonRunner(sys.executable)
    tests: list[dict] = []

    def record(name: str, passed: bool, detail: str) -> None:
        tests.append({"name": name, "status": "PASS" if passed else "FAIL", "detail": detail})

    success = runner.run_source(
        "values = [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]\n"
        "print('Hello from the lab')\n"
        "print('sum=', sum(values))\n"
        "print('average=', sum(values) / len(values))\n",
        root / "success",
        timeout_seconds=3,
    )
    record(
        "Test 1 — successful program",
        success.success and "sum= 30" in success.stdout and "average= 6.0" in success.stdout,
        success.status,
    )

    runtime = runner.run_source(
        "print(undefined_variable)\n",
        root / "runtime_error",
        timeout_seconds=3,
    )
    record(
        "Test 2 — runtime error capture",
        not runtime.success and "NameError" in runtime.stderr and runtime.phase == "execution",
        runtime.status,
    )

    syntax = runner.run_source(
        "def broken(:\n    pass\n",
        root / "syntax_error",
        timeout_seconds=3,
    )
    record(
        "Test 3 — syntax error capture",
        not syntax.success and syntax.phase == "syntax" and "SyntaxError" in syntax.stderr,
        syntax.status,
    )

    timeout = runner.run_source(
        "while True:\n    pass\n",
        root / "timeout",
        timeout_seconds=0.5,
    )
    record(
        "Test 4 — timeout",
        timeout.timed_out and timeout.status == "TIMED OUT",
        timeout.status,
    )

    repair_packet = ModelAdapter.build_repair_prompt(
        "Print the numbers 1 through 3.",
        "print(missing_name)\n",
        runtime.to_dict(),
        2,
        3,
    )
    required = ["ORIGINAL TASK", "PREVIOUS COMPLETE PROGRAM", "RETURN CODE", "STDERR", "ATTEMPT: 2 OF 3"]
    record(
        "Test 5A — automatic repair packet",
        all(token in repair_packet for token in required),
        "repair evidence packet complete",
    )
    return tests


def model_tests(root: Path) -> list[dict]:
    adapter = ModelAdapter()
    runner = PythonRunner(sys.executable)
    tests: list[dict] = []
    if not adapter.is_online():
        return [
            {
                "name": "Model tests",
                "status": "SKIP",
                "detail": "FOXAI Chat Engine is offline",
            }
        ]

    prompt = "Write a Python program that prints a greeting, creates five integers, and prints their sum and average."
    reply = adapter.generate_program(prompt)
    if not reply.ok:
        return [{"name": "Test 1M — model generation", "status": "FAIL", "detail": reply.error}]
    code = adapter.extract_candidate(reply.text)
    generated = runner.run_source(code, root / "model_success", timeout_seconds=8)
    tests.append(
        {
            "name": "Test 1M — model generation and execution",
            "status": "PASS" if generated.success else "FAIL",
            "detail": generated.status,
        }
    )

    bad = runner.run_source(
        "print(undefined_name)\n",
        root / "repair_initial_failure",
        timeout_seconds=3,
    )
    repaired_reply, repair_prompt = adapter.repair_program(
        "Write a program that prints the numbers 1 through 3 and their total.",
        "print(undefined_name)\n",
        bad.to_dict(),
        2,
        3,
    )
    (root / "repair_prompt.txt").write_text(repair_prompt, encoding="utf-8")
    if not repaired_reply.ok:
        tests.append(
            {
                "name": "Test 5M — automatic model repair",
                "status": "FAIL",
                "detail": repaired_reply.error,
            }
        )
    else:
        repaired_code = adapter.extract_candidate(repaired_reply.text)
        repaired = runner.run_source(repaired_code, root / "repair_corrected", timeout_seconds=8)
        tests.append(
            {
                "name": "Test 5M — automatic model repair",
                "status": "PASS" if (not bad.success and repaired.success) else "FAIL",
                "detail": f"first={bad.status}, repaired={repaired.status}",
            }
        )
    return tests


def layout_test() -> list[dict]:
    missing = sorted(name for name in EXPECTED_FILES if not (LAB_ROOT / name).is_file())
    return [
        {
            "name": "Layout — required files",
            "status": "PASS" if not missing else "FAIL",
            "detail": "all required files present" if not missing else "missing: " + ", ".join(missing),
        },
        {
            "name": "Layout — lab root",
            "status": "PASS" if LAB_ROOT.name == "DirtyPythonLab" and LAB_ROOT.parent.name == "_LAB" else "FAIL",
            "detail": str(LAB_ROOT),
        },
    ]


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--deterministic-only", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument("--with-model", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument("--layout-only", action="store_true")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    RUNS.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    run_id = "SELF_TEST_" + datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    test_root = RUNS / run_id
    test_root.mkdir(parents=True)

    before = protected_fingerprints()
    tests = layout_test()
    if not args.layout_only:
        tests.extend(deterministic_tests(test_root))
        if args.with_model and not args.deterministic_only:
            tests.extend(model_tests(test_root))
    after = protected_fingerprints()
    isolation_ok = before == after
    tests.append(
        {
            "name": "Test 6 — production isolation",
            "status": "PASS" if isolation_ok else "FAIL",
            "detail": "core, ui, and site-packages metadata unchanged" if isolation_ok else "protected-path metadata changed",
        }
    )

    report = {
        "schema": "foxai.dirty_python_lab.acceptance.v1",
        "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
        "python": sys.executable,
        "lab_root": str(LAB_ROOT),
        "protected_before": before,
        "protected_after": after,
        "tests": tests,
    }
    (test_root / "acceptance_report.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(report, indent=2), encoding="utf-8"
    )

    for test in tests:
        print(result_line(test["name"], test["status"], test["detail"]))

    failures = [test for test in tests if test["status"] == "FAIL"]
    print()
    print(f"RESULT: {'PASS' if not failures else 'FAIL'}")
    print(f"Report: {test_root / 'acceptance_report.json'}")
    return 0 if not failures else 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

FOXAI DIRTY PYTHON LAB V1
Prompt, Generate, Run, Capture, Repair

START
Double-click:
START_DIRTY_PYTHON_LAB.bat

NORMAL USE
1. Type what you want the Python program to do.
2. Click GENERATE AND TEST.
3. The lab writes, runs, captures errors, and attempts repair automatically.
4. Read SUCCESS, FAILED, TIMED OUT, STOPPED, or MODEL OFFLINE in the large status area.

BUTTONS
GENERATE AND TEST
Creates and tests a program. Up to three attempts are allowed.

STOP
Stops the current local-model request when possible and terminates the running Python process tree.

OPEN WORKSPACE
Opens the latest candidate.py, prompt.txt, result.json, and model response folder.

RESET WORKSPACE
Clears only the current workspace. Historical run folders are preserved.

RUN SELF TESTS
Runs success, runtime-error, syntax-error, timeout, repair-packet, model integration when available, and production-isolation checks.

FILES
Current convenient copies:
Z:\FOXAI\_LAB\DirtyPythonLab\workspace

Historical attempts:
Z:\FOXAI\_LAB\DirtyPythonLab\runs

MODEL ROUTE
Default API:
http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions

Default health check:
http://127.0.0.1:8080/health

The route can be changed later with FOXAI_CHAT_API and FOXAI_CHAT_HEALTH environment variables.

BOUNDARY
This is an owner-operated disposable workspace, not a hardened Windows security sandbox.
Generated code is instructed to stay inside the workspace. It runs without administrator elevation, with shell=False, a timeout, no package installation, and no automatic copy into FOXAI production folders.

The lab does not modify packages in:
Z:\FOXAI\env

The acceptance test fingerprints:
Z:\FOXAI\core
Z:\FOXAI\ui
Z:\FOXAI\env\python\Lib\site-packages

Workshop remains the only route for promoting a tested change into production FOXAI.
